texto: Men go to the ballpark with an assumed knowledge and interest, whereas women need to constantly demonstrate how much they know and care.

frase_original: Men go to the ballpark with an assumed knowledge and interest, whereas women need to constantly demonstrate how much they know and care.

texto: We habitually compare ourselves to others to a debilitating degree, believing our successes can only be captured by how much we've outpaced someone else. We deal in acceptable ideas. We disregard our own capabilities. We waste a lot of time and emotion on what everyone else is doing well or badly, when we should be investing in and celebrating ourselves. And sometimes we simply forget that we like our own company, or that we love things for our own, deeply personal, individualistic reasons.In short, we forget ourselves, and how to be alone.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Stacey May Fowles: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “We habitually compare ourselves to others to a debilitating degree, believing our successes can o…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, solitude, ourselves pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
